and Akash (space, ether). Their duration in thb sequence is 6,
12, 18, 24 and 30 minutes. Jala (water) and Vayu (air, wind)
are faminine. The others are masculine. This is a slight modi
fication of the theory given in Uttara Kalamrita. This text has
fixed for ever -the same sequence of the Tattvas as follows in
Vighatis:
Duration Tattva Sex
=
0-15 6 minutes Prithivi Male
=
16-45 12 minutes Jala Female
=
46-90 18 minutes Tejas Male
=
91-150 24 minutes Vayu Female
=
151-225 30 minutes Akash Male
Here the sequence is made to depend on the weekday
alone. Even our major objection remains. After Akash Tattva
we get the Prithivi Tattva. During these 36 minutes there
should be no female births. This is simply impossible. Another
gives birth to twins - a boy and a girl - within the duration of
the same Tattva or within the Akash and Prithvi Tattvas. The
sex determination dependent on the Tattvas fails miserably.
As we noticed earlier, one way to overcome the difficulty is to
